item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made some tweaks to the first sentence that are almost but not quite totally different to your suggestion. Uh mostly I didn't like the way "Chicago, Illinois, and…" flowed so I refactored it so the second comma there wasn't needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, "Foundation facilities" is a bit redundant, right? So you could just go

Instances of SCP-4518 reside in Adapted Storage Facility-03, a converted pub located in Chicago, Illinois, and Adapted Storage Facility-04, a converted storage area located in Site-17.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you enjoyed it :3

I've fixed the highlighted run-on sentence; and one in the containment procedures. The first sentence also feels overlong to me but I don't really know a good way to make it shorter :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
love those good good mcelroy boys! I had seen your opening note before reading (probably would not have caught the reference otherwise), so in my mind all of the comedians were Griffins, which only made the darker turn that much more tragic — I'm not sure the world could survive such a sustained loss of media luminaries even if virtually all of them had just been spontaneously generated

to the actual SCP: The timeline is well-paced and does a nice job of handling the hahaha-oh-wait-oh-no shift the narrative takes. I love the fridge-horror detail that it takes the Ethics Committee less than half an hour to terminate so much sentient life (and innocent bystanders), and that such a meeting and decision is so nonurgent as to receive hardly more emphasis than the details of the misinformation campaign.

Speaking of: my only nitpicks would be fairly minor grammatical/run-on sentence stuff. Like:

Following these events, a Foundation misinformation campaign was run to spread news of there having been a bar fight that got out of hand at the venue with property damage revealing the presence of asbestos, leading to the venue being sealed because of public health concerns relating to potential squatters.

This is… just a lot of stacked clauses in a single sentence. Separating at that last comma instead with a "… presence of asbestos. According to the campaign, this led to the venue…" adds a bit more language but to my mind clarifies things tremendously.

Regardless — good stuff, you cool baby. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP feels familiar… but not too familiar. But not too not familiar. +1

I love the transition from "oh, this is pretty goofy" to "oh, that's super dark." Great work!

Super weird fun fact: I was actually listening to a live MBMBaM show when I read this.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded: Hey, thanks for the kind words :3 I'm glad you enjoyed it so much :3

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded:
Very nice! I realize this has already got some crit, but I just felt the need to go into why I dig this so much.

Tanhony rambles
TURN IT OFF

A few things I especially liked here:

No monitoring of the interior of either of these facilities is to be carried out.

I’m a sucker for containment procedures that at first seem to go against the most basic forms of containment. It leaves me wondering why we can’t observe what’s going on here, which is of course the point, and just makes me want to know exactly what’s going on here even more.

MTF Iota-10 requested a large number of transport vans to move the SCP-4518 instances back to Site 17.

There’s a wonderful sense of dramatic irony here, with all the efforts Iota-10 are taking to manage the absurd numbers of 4518’s only creating more of them and making their job even harder. We can see that this is a serious situation, but it can’t help but be a little humorous as well.

The entrances to the venue were sealed with concrete blocks; with an unknown number of police and patrons still inside.

I feel like I got whiplash here in the best way possible. Suddenly, this isn’t funny at all. If this tone shift wasn’t taken care of properly, it could come across as jarring, but the way it’s done here just makes your stomach drop.

Incineration is to be done at a minimum temperature of 1500°C for at least four hours, with the ashes that remain being passed through a grinder to ensure none of the remains are identifiable.

The last line here, finally clarifying what the ‘biological matter’ that’s been referred to throughout the piece is, is a fantastic note to end on. It’s going to stick with me for a good while, I can say that much.

A hearty +1!

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to Tanhony or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Okay I've refactored the description a little; is that clearer?

I don't necessarily want to make the clothing thing be too much of a big deal cos it's a relatively minor part of the article.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: You know what, I never even thought of them changing clothes, so that makes more sense. It could probably be phrased better but I could've just not been thinking about it right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I respectfully disagree with this statement:

Even better if you gave this figure a name or described the anomaly as being a child or adolescent entity to further twist the proverbial knife in the audience that, no matter how old you are, the Foundation is willing to go that extra mile just to uphold its own values.

This is already shown very well in this article from this statement:

The entrances to the venue were sealed with concrete blocks1; with an unknown number of police and patrons still inside.

This communicates that the Foundation not only sealed authorities, but civilians in an attempt to maintain secrecy and normalcy. There is a certain kind of horror of being trapped with a self-replicating person. There is another kind of horror in knowing that you can't get out, and there is only so much room and food left. Children need not be involved to be able to get that across.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Hey, thanks for the crit here :3 I've cleaned up most of the things.

+ quick follow up
- quick follow up

Also wouldn't they all be wearing the exact same thing?

In the events described, yes; but they can change clothing and indeed I would expect the Foundation to provide clean clothing occasionally that isn't necessarily the same.

This said, I'm open to changing this anyway? It might flow better without but it feels to me like it's a somewhat important detail.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Hey cybersqyd,
Here Be Crit / Opinion
ByeBye

Nitpicks:

SCP-4518 instances are not immune to this and can cause the number of instances present to increase by counting themselves, even when only a single instance is present.

This sticks out sour to me, specifically "SCP-4518 instances are not immune to this..", this makes it seem like the previous statement wasn't already referring to SCP-4518, could be solved by just removing that phrase, this also could just be a personal preference thing.

SCP-4518 instances are visually identical, approximately 30 year old white males, with newly appearing instances being dressed in the same clothing as an existing instance nearby.

I feel like this sentence belong right after the first sentence of the description if not combined with that first sentence. Also wouldn't they all be wearing the exact same thing? If so the " as an existing instance nearby." seems unnecessary.

This was rapidly followed by many duplications as the audience saw what happened.

Instead of "many" maybe "multiple" would sound better.

The venue started to experience severe over-crowding, which lead to the venue owner phoning the police to clear out the venue.

Could just be me but "phoning" feels a tad nonclinical, maybe "contact" or something like it would work better.

At this point, multiple SCP-4518 instances had spilled out onto the street.

Spilled out seems a tad nonclinical as well, "relocated" or something to that effect.

..it was determined that the current supply levels for both sites was insufficient to handle the populations of SCP-4518 being contained there.

I think "was" should be "were".

Opinion: Okay little nitpicks aside I like this very much. In my opinion, it's a fantastic example of a simple idea done right. The subtle nod to MBMBaM is well done and doesn't try to overpower the article. While being a tad dark the overall situational humor of this really worked well for me. An easy +1.



Site crit : Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to DrAkimoto or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I think you're right; I added one and it looks better.

That said, I chose to not use a child/adolescent because I felt it'd parse badly considering they're meant to be doing a comedy show at a bar?

[EDIT: Okay so I was actually considering whether to make deeper changes so that'd work; cos e.g, they can run on stage mid-performance and narratively it'd work. That said, I think front-loading with it being a child would setup the horror too much: people would immediately suspect bad things and the whiplash from the discovery initially being almost slapstick in nature to it suddenly being dark feels like it would be less effective overall.

Plus I feel like it'd feel gratuitous? It wouldn't feel like there's a satisfying reason for it being a child other than because a child is more horror?]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you could've hit the nail on the head even harder if you gave this article a picture to accompany it. A picture of what the individual looks like so that the humanization emphasizes the Foundation's inhumane efforts in order to uphold its own directives.

Even better if you gave this figure a name or described the anomaly as being a child or adolescent entity to further twist the proverbial knife in the audience that, no matter how old you are, the Foundation is willing to go that extra mile just to uphold its own values.

It just feels like with all of this, the article could finally flourish in its true potential, but in its current form, it offers little in the way of serviceable articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey so this is my first SCP. It was born out of a joke about how there's always one more brother in MBMBaM than you expect; and I started toying with the idea of a SCP who's anomalous property is that it duplicates upon being counted. Originally, this was gonna feature an interview with the SCP replicating but then I realised something: how do you get one instance alone in a room?

This was originally gonna be a lot lighter but it didn't feel like it'd work as well as it does in the current form, where you have the relatively light exploration log and then the very dark ending.

Big thanks to RockTeethMothEyes, gee0765, DrAkimoto and Tanhony for critting this; and tupacofficial, MaliceAforethought and MacWarren for help with the conceptual drafting. Also thanks to my wife who had to keep putting up with me thinking through this at them.

Image source is here

ETA: hey this is part of the Site 17 Deepwell Catalog now! Go have a poke around cos Nagiros is doing cool shit with it! :3

+ is this complex enough to need spoilers?
- you can have them anyway
  • The Foundation can't go into the storage facilities
  • Biological matter is SCP-4518 instances
  • Sorry this is dark
  • The discovery log very implicitly (noting that I did not do any research to make sure this is consistent) takes place at the same bar as the first MBMBaM live show and also on that date. This is the only surviving nod to the podcast. I killed the others.
